California · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board under the California Department of Consumer Affairs (DCA). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$150
$35 RPE application + $115 permanent license application
Renewal
$150
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 6 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board

Mailing Address

2005 Evergreen St., Suite 2100, Sacramento, CA 95815

Physical Address

2005 Evergreen St., Suite 2100, Sacramento, CA 95815

Phone & Fax

Fax: (916) 263-2668

Email

speechandhearing@dca.ca.gov

Credential Categories

California SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in California. Requires a master's degree, completion of Required Professional Experience (RPE), and a passing Praxis score.

RPE Temporary License

Temporary license issued to SLP graduates completing their Required Professional Experience (RPE). Valid for 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time under supervision of a licensed SLP.

Application Process

How to Apply for a California Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree or equivalent from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program. Must include 300 hours of supervised clinical practicum across at least three different clinical settings.

Obtain an RPE Temporary License

Apply for the RPE temporary license ($35 fee) to begin your Required Professional Experience. Complete 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time of supervised clinical practice under a licensed SLP.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331). The current passing score is 162. Score must be no more than five years old at the time of application.

Apply for Permanent Licensure

Submit the permanent license application with the remaining $115 fee. Include official transcripts, RPE completion verification, Praxis score report, and fingerprint background check results.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331. Must be dated within the last five years.
  • RPE completion verificationSigned by supervising licensed SLP confirming 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time.
  • Fingerprint background checkLive Scan fingerprinting submitted to the Department of Justice and FBI.
Telepractice

California Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

California permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid California SLP license to provide services to clients located in California, regardless of the practitioner's physical location. All telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person services.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

California issues an RPE Temporary License for graduates completing their Required Professional Experience (RPE). This allows supervised practice for 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time. The RPE supervisor must hold a current California SLP license.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

California offers alternative licensure pathways for out-of-state SLPs. Applicants holding a current ASHA CCC-SLP or a valid license in another state may qualify for expedited review. However, California does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. All out-of-state applicants must submit a full application with California-specific documentation.

Frequently Asked Questions

California SLP FAQ

How much does a California SLP license cost?

The total initial licensure cost is $150. This includes a $35 RPE temporary license application fee and $115 for the permanent license application. Biennial renewal is $150.

Does California accept the ASHA CCC-SLP for licensure?

Holding the ASHA CCC-SLP provides an alternative pathway to California SLP licensure. CCC-SLP holders may qualify for expedited review, but must still submit a full California application with all required documentation and fees.

Can I practice telepractice as an SLP in California?

Yes. California permits licensed SLPs to provide telepractice services. You must hold a valid California SLP license to treat clients located in California, and all telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person treatment.
